sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard (formerly CAC/RS 75-1976) shall apply to quick-frozen peaches of the species Prunus persica L. as defined below and offered for direct consumption without further processing, except repacking, if required. It does not apply to the product when indicated as intended for further processing or for other industrial purposes.

sections.text: Quick-frozen peaches is the product prepared from fresh, sound, properly ripened fruit conforming to the characteristics of Prunus persica L., but excluding nectarine varieties, which fruit is packed with or without a dry sugar of a syrup and is packaged.

sections.title: Process definition
sections.text: Quick-frozen peaches is the product subjected to a freezing process in appropriate equipment and complying with the conditions laid down hereafter. This freezing operation shall be carried out in such a way that the range of temperature of maximum crystallization is passed quickly. The quick-freezing process shall not be regarded as complete unless and until the product temperature has reached -18 dcC (0 dcF) at the thermal centre after thermal stabilization. The recognized practice of repacking quick-frozen products under controlled conditions is permitted.

sections.title: Handling practice
sections.text: The product shall be handled under such conditions as will maintain the quality during transportation, storage and distribution up to and including the time of final sale. It is recommended that during storage, transportation, distribution and retail, the product be handled in accordance with the provisions in the Code of Practice for the Processing and Handling of Quick Frozen Foods (CXC 8-1976).1

sections.title: Colour type
sections.text: Peaches of distinct varietal differences shall be designated according to the colour of the ripe flesh: (a) white ndash; varietal types in which the predominant colour ranges from white to yellow-white; (b) yellow ndash; varietal types in which the predominant colour ranges from pale yellow to light range; (c) red ndash; varietal types in which the colour ranges from orange red to red with more or less pronounced variegated red colouring other than that associated with the pit cavity; and (d) green ndash; varietal types in which the predominant colour is light green, but which are fully mature and properly ripened.

sections.title: Style
sections.text: Quick-frozen peaches shall be presented in the following styles: (a) whole ndash; unpitted whole peaches; (b) halves ndash; pitted and cut into two approximately equal parts; (c) quarters ndash; pitted and cut into four approximately equal parts following the longitudinal axis; (d) sliced ndash; pitted and cut into wedge-shaped sectors of approximately equal size; (e) pieces ndash; (regular or irregular) ndash; pitted and comprising regular or irregular shapes and sizes; and (f) diced ndash; pitted and cut into cube-like parts having a maximum size of 15 mm long on one edge.

sections.title: General requirements
sections.text: Quick-frozen peaches shall be: (a) clean and practically free from foreign material; (b) free from foreign flavour and odour; (c) of similar varietal characteristics; and (d) of good, reasonably uniform colour characteristic of the varietal type. and with respect to visual or other defects subject to a tolerance, shall be: (e) practically free from dark discolouration or green areas (except for green in green types); (f) practically free from blemished units; (g) practically free from stalks (stems), or portions thereof, or other extraneous vegetable matter (EVM); (h) practically intact units for the style and may be materially altered in shape due to e xcess trimming or mechanical damage; (i) reasonably free from fibrous units; (j) reasonably free from overripe, mushy or disintegrated fruit; (k) reasonably free from peel; (l) free from whole pits (stones) except in whole style; and (m) practically free from pit fragments.

sections.title: Definition of visual defects
sections.text: (a) Discolouration – discolouration due to oxidation or bruising and which materially detracts from the appearance of the product; (b) Blemish – units affected by insect injury or scab pathological damage or other similar means; (c) Stalks (stems) – the immediate stalk which attaches the peach to the branch of the peach tree; (d) Extraneous Vegetable Matter (EVM) – harmless vegetable material such as pieces of leaf; (e) Excess trim and mechanical damage  – units gouged or severely trimmed such that the apparent appearance and shape of the unit is materially altered;

sections.title: 3.3.3 Standard sample sizes
sections.text:
The sample size for segregating, classifying and enumerating visual defects is as follows:

Style       Standard sample size

Whole       20 units (whole fruits)
Halved and quartered   30 units (halves and quarters)
Sliced, diced, pieces   300 grams

The above sample units are made up of drained fruit ( see relevant Codex texts on methods of analysis and sampling).

sections.title: Definition of "defective" for quality factors
sections.text:
Any sample unit taken in accordance with an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5, (see relevant Codex texts on methods of analysis and sampling), and which is adjusted to a standard sample unit size for applying the tolerances relating to visual defects, shall be regarded as "defective" for the respective characteristics, as follows: 
(a) any sample unit that fails to meet the general requirements of Section 3.3.1; and 
(b) any sample unit that fails the total allowable points for defect categories minor, major, or serious; or which fails the total allowable points for the combined total of the respective defect categories (3.3.4).

sentences.original: Peaches of distinct varietal differences shall be designated according to the colour of the ripe flesh: (a) white ndash; varietal types in which the predominant colour ranges from white to yellow-white; (b) yellow ndash; varietal types in which the predominant colour ranges from pale yellow to light range; (c) red ndash; varietal types in which the colour ranges from orange red to red with more or less pronounced variegated red colouring other than that associated with the pit cavity; and (d) green ndash; varietal types in which the predominant colour is light green, but which are fully mature and properly ripened.

sentences.original: Quick-frozen peaches shall be presented in the following styles: (a) whole ndash; unpitted whole peaches; (b) halves ndash; pitted and cut into two approximately equal parts; (c) quarters ndash; pitted and cut into four approximately equal parts following the longitudinal axis; (d) sliced ndash; pitted and cut into wedge-shaped sectors of approximately equal size; (e) pieces ndash; (regular or irregular) ndash; pitted and comprising regular or irregular shapes and sizes; and (f) diced ndash; pitted and cut into cube-like parts having a maximum size of 15 mm long on one edge.

sentences.original:
Any sample unit taken in accordance with an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5, (see relevant Codex texts on methods of analysis and sampling), and which is adjusted to a standard sample unit size for applying the tolerances relating to visual defects, shall be regarded as "defective" for the respective characteristics, as follows: 
(a) any sample unit that fails to meet the general requirements of Section 3.3.1; and 
(b) any sample unit that fails the total allowable points for defect categories minor, major, or serious; or which fails the total allowable points for the combined total of the respective defect categories (3.3.4).

pages.summary: A página apresenta um título principal em caixa alta 'STANDARD FOR QUICK-FROZEN PEACHES' com um cabeçalho repetitivo 'CXS 75-1981'. Há um texto associado explicativo sobre adoção e emendas. Não há subdivisões ou continuação para outras páginas.
Página com cabeçalho repetitivo e um título principal '2022 Amendment' em caixa alta nível 1. Texto explicativo associado ao título. Ausência de subdivisões ou continuidade para outras páginas.

pages.title: CXS 75-1981
pages.summary: A página 3 do documento CXS 75-1981 apresenta o cabeçalho repetitivo 'CXS 75-1981  3' e contém duas seções principais numeradas: '1. SCOPE' e '2. DESCRIPTION'. A seção 1 contém texto explicativo, enquanto a seção 2 é um agrupador de subseções numeradas (2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4). A subseção 2.4 por sua vez contém quatro subdivisões numéricas (2.4.1 a 2.4.4) com seus respectivos textos explicativos. Não há indicações de continuidade de conteúdo com páginas anteriores ou seguintes. A estrutura hierárquica segue a numeração apresentada no texto e os títulos estão visíveis na página.

A página 4 do documento CXS 75-1981 apresenta uma estrutura clara de seções numeradas, com a seção 3 como tópico principal contendo as subseções 3.1, 3.2 e 3.3, que por sua vez subdividem em níveis hierárquicos inferiores. O texto está associado corretamente aos seus respectivos títulos, que estão visíveis na página. A seção 3 e a subseção 3.3 indicam continuidade para a próxima página sem fechamento definitivo do conteúdo, especialmente a subseção 3.3.2, que continua na página seguinte.

pages.title: CXS 75-1981
pages.summary: A página apresenta a continuação da seção 3.3 iniciada na página anterior, com subseções 3.3.3 e 3.3.4 claramente identificadas. A subseção 3.3.3 contém texto explicativo detalhado dos tamanhos padrão de amostra. A subseção 3.3.4 inclui uma tabela com tolerâncias para defeitos. Há indicação clara de continuidade da seção 3.3 e da subseção 3.3.4 para a próxima página. O título principal da página é o código do documento, e o cabeçalho inclui o código e a paginação.

pages.title: CXS 75-1981
pages.summary: Página contendo tabelas detalhadas sobre tolerâncias para defeitos de amostras em estilos 'halved and quartered' e 'sliced, diced, pieces'. Seções principais 3.3.4 e 3.3.5 claramente demarcadas, com continuidade de 3.3.4 da página anterior e segue para a próxima. Adequado para RAG com associação correta de textos e títulos.

A página apresenta seções estruturadas numeradas do documento, iniciando em 3.3.6 com texto explicativo. Seguem seções principais 4, 5 e 6 com seus respectivos subtítulos numerados, contendo texto associado correto. A hierarquia respeita os níveis 1 a 3 conforme numeração. Nenhuma seção parece continuar de página anterior ou continuar para próxima página nesta página. Cabeçalho repetitivo é identificado e excluído dos textos dos nós. Estrutura adequada para uso em RAG.

Página contém seções numeradas 6.4, 7 e 8 como nível 1, com texto associado correto. A seção 7 tem subitens (a) a (d) apresentados como nível 2 com os textos como títulos curtos e sem texto interno. Não há continuidade entre páginas para estas seções. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado e excluído do conteúdo textual dos nós. Estrutura bem definida para uso em RAG.
